title: The Road North
description: Encounters on the road to the Whispering Cave: clever slimes, Grut the goblin merchant, wolf pack standoff.

lines: Between Harmonia and the Whispering Cave lay two hours of open road and at least three things that wanted to hurt them.
The first thing was a pair of slimes. Not ordinary slimes — these were sharper, more deliberate in their malice.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Oh. Oh, that's clever.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: The slime is clever?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The DESIGN is clever. Don't hit it with the sword again. Use fire. Area effects. Force them to converge rather than divide.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: I would have preferred simply stabbing them.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You can simply stab the next thing. I promise there will be a next thing.

steps.text: The next thing was a goblin named, according to a hand-painted sign, GRUT — AUTHENTIC HERO SUPPLIES.

lines.speaker: Grut
lines.text: Greetings, heroes! You look like you are going to the cave. Good news. Grut has everything you need.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: That helmet is a pot.

lines.speaker: Grut
lines.text: Authentic ancient warrior vessel! Very rare. Very powerful. Kings wore these.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: What king wore a pot on his head?

lines.speaker: Grut
lines.text: ...King Pothelm. Very famous king. From the west. You wouldn't have heard of him.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You will not.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The story requires that we purchased something from the goblin merchant. The narrative demands it.

lines.speaker: Grut
lines.text: Good luck in the cave! If you die, your equipment reverts to the shop system! Very convenient!

steps.text: No one knew what the shop system was. They kept walking.
The third obstacle was a pack of wolves. The Bard pulled her lute from her back and began to play.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Bard performance and Cleric radiant aura. Combined effect on territorial mammalian aggression: decisive. The system works better when we coordinate.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: That's what I've been saying.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You've been saying it in verse. It lands differently in prose.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Is it just me, or did that fight feel... scripted? Not our lines. Our movements. Like someone wrote the choreography before we arrived.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Efficient choreography is not the same as scripted choreography.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Isn't it, though?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: We've fought a lot of things today. More than most parties would. Is anyone else noticing that we're... stronger than the road seems to expect?

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Practice makes better.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Practice makes the story wonder why you're still in the first chapter.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: That last fight. The way you found the angle — the one the monsters weren't guarding. Does anyone else feel like the world has... seams? Places where the design doesn't quite connect?

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Seams are just opportunities with bad PR.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I'm going to write that down. For the song. The one I'm definitely writing about all of this.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Every fight we chose ourselves. Every swing, every spell, every decision — ours. There's something honest about that. Something the road seems to... respect.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Roads don't respect things.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: This one does. I can hear it in the way the whispers change when we're coming. They expected something. We're not it.

steps.text: They reached the cave mouth as the sun dropped behind the mountains and the whispers began.
